channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Data-driven startup Aggie uses AI to manage small business social media
item.description: A new, AI-powered platform called Aggie wants to manage your brand’s social media. Aggie is a product of Audience Genomics, which has since 2018 offered audience data analytics to big brands like Universal Studios, AMC Television, and Fenty Beauty.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: FCC Chairman Appointed Amid Controversy Over Trump's Influence
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump appoints Brendan Carr as new FCC chairman amidst controversy over White House control and Trump's influence on social media regulation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency: A Closer Look
item.description: Elon Musk's plans for efficiency in government, his approach to cost-cutting, and potential conflicts of interest
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Trump team is seeking to ease US Rules for self-driving cars
item.description: Current federal rules pose significant roadblocks for companies looking to launch vehicles without steering wheels or pedals in large quantities, which Tesla intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: Most influencers — 63% — are men and the majority — 77% — have no affiliation, or background, with a media organization.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: A report released Monday by Pew Research Center says about one in five Americans regularly get their news from so-called news influencers on social media.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: From AI boom to AI bottlenecks
item.description: Ivo Ivanov, CEO at DE-CIX discusses how why some enterprises struggle to achieve viable returns on their AI investments.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Prosegur se apoya en la IA para optimizar y agilizar los procesos de selección
item.description: Desde su constitución en el año 1976, la compañía de seguridad Prosegur ha basado su crecimiento y evolución en una estrategia sustentada en tres pilares fundamentales: la innovación, su equipo humano y una orientación al cliente.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Designing the APIs that accidentally power businesses
item.description: In the last decade, every web application developer has become an API designer. But most organizations do not think of developers as API designers, nor do they think about APIs as a product of design — to the detriment of their developers’ productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The dirty little secret of open source contributions
item.description: “Nobody cares if you contribute.” That’s what a Postgres friend said to me during lunch at KubeCon when I suggested that hiring Postgres contributors could be a selling point for customers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia is planning to block TikTok and Instagram for kids under 16
item.description: How do you remove children from the harms of social media? Politically the answer appears simple in Australia, but practically the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Getting started with Google Password Manager
item.description: If you’re still trying to remember all of your passwords and then type ’em into sites by hand, let me tell you: You’re doing it wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Strategies to navigate the pitfalls of cloud costs
item.description: In my line of work, I often hear CIOs and CFOs say, “Cloud providers are bleeding us dry.” As ever-increasing cloud bills roll in, they’re demanding answers about what they’re paying for and why they’re paying so much. Public cloud providers are eager to oppose this line of questioning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cruise admits to filing false report to sway federal probe into San Francisco crash
item.description: The autonomous car company did not include details about a woman being dragged over 20 feet by one of its self-driving vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Jason Green, The Mercury News

item.title: JetBrains IDEs ease debugging for Kubernetes apps
item.description: JetBrains IDEs, with new 2024.3 edition releases, now provide a logical code structure view alongside the familiar physical code structure view in the Structure tool window.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Only 8 monkeys remain free after more than a week outside a South Carolina compound
item.description: Authorities say Just eight monkeys remain free from the group who broke out of a South Carolina compound that breeds the primates for medical research.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Solar-powered aircraft achieves groundbreaking 22-hour autonomous flight
item.description: Skydweller is the world's largest unmanned solar-powered aircraft that can stay airborne for weeks, and even months, without refueling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the floating Android note app of the future
item.description: Time for a brutal truth any productivity connoisseur can confirm: At a certain point, once you’ve seen one note-taking app, you’ve kinda seen ’em all. I mean, sure: We’ve got some commendably compelling choices when it comes to note-taking apps on Android.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use DispatchProxy for AOP in .NET Core
item.description: Aspect-oriented programming (AOP) decomposes an application’s source code into distinct aspects that isolate the core business logic from cross-cutting concerns.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Understanding Hyperlight, Microsoft’s minimal VM manager
item.description: Virtualization seemed to be one of those technologies that was done. With Hyper-V, VMware, and KVM all offering similar features and performance, all that was left to do was to support new chipset instructions and fix bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, has moved to the release candidate stage. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At CBP, automated software testing is integral to the mission
item.description: Some parts of the system are more difficult to automate, including operations like biometric fingerprinting. The post At CBP, automated software testing is integral to the mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alarming rise of fake legal requests: What it means for your privacy
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says there’s been a rise in cybercriminal services using hacked police and government emails to send subpoenas and data requests to U.S. companies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, NewsNation
